campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help fund scholarships for kids in the city's recreation programs. They also discussed moving forward with plans to build a bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including submitting a grant application to help pay for it.
keyTopics: Water Tower Kitchen donates $4,000 to youth recreation scholarships
Council considering extension of interim ordinance #2326
Submitting grant application for Hamilton Avenue bicycle/pedestrian bridge over Highway 17
Final feasibility study approved for Highway 17 overcrossing project

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents had a chance to speak about a proposed new rule or regulation. After hearing public input, the council moved to fast-track the approval process by skipping the normal first reading step and planning to vote on the final adoption at the next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident input on a new ordinance
Council approved expedited approval process (waived first reading)
Ordinance scheduled for final vote at next regular council meeting
Details of the specific ordinance not specified in available agenda materials

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from several March sessions and authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda was primarily administrative, focused on formally documenting previous meetings and moving forward with a construction project.
keyTopics: Approval of meeting minutes from multiple March 2026 sessions
Construction contract authorization with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given authority to execute the construction agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 21, 2026
san-jose.summary: San José City Council met to approve several service agreements and projects. The main items included renewing a contract with Vanderlande Industries to keep the airport's baggage system running, addressing affordable housing protections when subsidy contracts end, updating the storm sewer system master plan, and moving forward with the FEMA Family Camp project.
keyTopics: Airport baggage handling system contract renewal
Affordable housing protections for at-risk residents
Storm sewer system planning and upgrades
FEMA Family Camp project approval
Committee meeting minutes approval

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: Mountain View's city council met to discuss the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, including which infrastructure projects and improvements the city will fund. The council also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and volunteers in the community.
keyTopics: 2026-27 budget for city construction and infrastructure projects
Teen Month proclamation
National Volunteer Week recognition

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 7, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held their regular meeting on April 7, 2026, where they recognized Arab-American Heritage Month and Fair Housing Month. The council opened with the traditional flag salute and featured these observances as special items on their agenda.
keyTopics: Arab-American Heritage Month recognition
Fair Housing Month observance
Regular city council meeting proceedings

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a community workshop on April 13, 2026, to teach residents how to use OpenGov, a tool that lets you see how the city spends your tax money. The council also discussed rules requiring people who lobby the city—trying to influence decisions on laws or policies—to register and report what they're doing.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool training for residents
New lobbyist registration and reporting requirements
City budget transparency and spending oversight
Rules for people attempting to influence city decisions

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included important legal information for residents. If you disagree with a city decision and want to sue or legally challenge it, you have 90 days from when the decision is finalized to file your lawsuit, or you'll lose your right to challenge it.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to file lawsuits against city decisions
Legal rights for challenging city council rulings
California Civil Procedure code requirements
How to protect your legal interests in city matters

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review and update several planning documents and policies. The main focus was on approving changes to how the city uses federal housing and community development funds, updating council procedures for handling vacancies, and organizing the schedule for upcoming meetings.
keyTopics: Changes to how federal housing and community development money will be spent over the next 5 years
Amendment to the rules about filling empty City Council seats
Review of new projects and initiatives requested by individual council members
Approval of previous meeting minutes and scheduling for future meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a historic building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown. The owners want to upgrade the building's historic status from Category 4 to Category 2, which would allow them to add 5,000 square feet of new floor space as a reward for preserving and seismically strengthening the historic structure.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic building upgrade request
5,000 sq ft bonus floor area for seismic safety improvements
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule and assignments
Downtown commercial district development incentives
